hero.name: Erwann Lesech
hero.title: Data & AI Engineer
hero.tagline: I design data pipelines and machine-learning systems that turn raw signals into reliable products — from ingestion to inference, in production.
hero.graph_hint: Hover or tap a sphere to explore a skill
hero.panel_default_text: Each sphere is a skill I work with, grouped by domain and color. Hover or click one to see how I use it and which projects it powers.

about.title: About
about.bio: I'm a Data & AI Engineer who enjoys living at the intersection of solid data engineering and applied machine learning. I care about systems that are observable, reproducible, and genuinely useful — not just demos. Outside of building, I write about LLM evaluation, streaming architectures, and the ethics of AI.

bullets: Built streaming and batch pipelines (Spark, Kafka, Airflow) processing tens of millions of events per day.
Shipped a production RAG assistant on top of internal docs with FastAPI and a vector store, deployed on Kubernetes.
Introduced infrastructure-as-code with Terraform and standardized CI/CD for ML workloads.
Mentored two junior engineers on data modeling and MLOps practices.
Developed computer-vision models for anomaly detection with PyTorch and scikit-learn.
Containerized training and inference workloads with Docker on GCP.
Built internal dashboards in React to expose model metrics to researchers.
Explored transformer architectures for French-language text classification.
Co-authored a technical report on data augmentation for low-resource NLP.
Implemented reproducible experiment tracking for the team.

llm-rag.title: LLM RAG Assistant
llm-rag.description: A retrieval-augmented assistant that answers questions over private documents with citations.
llm-rag.details: An end-to-end RAG system: document ingestion and chunking, embeddings stored in a vector database, a FastAPI inference layer with streaming responses, and a Next.js chat UI. Includes an evaluation harness to track answer faithfulness and retrieval quality over time. Deployed on AWS with autoscaling on Kubernetes.

streaming-pipeline.title: Real-time Streaming Pipeline
streaming-pipeline.description: An event-driven data platform ingesting and transforming millions of events per day.
streaming-pipeline.details: Kafka handles ingestion, Spark Structured Streaming performs transformations, and Airflow orchestrates downstream batch jobs. Infrastructure is fully managed with Terraform on AWS, with monitoring and data-quality checks at every stage.

vision-anomaly.title: Vision Anomaly Detection
vision-anomaly.description: A computer-vision model that flags defects on production lines in near real time.
vision-anomaly.details: Built with PyTorch and classical scikit-learn baselines, packaged in Docker and served on GCP. The system combines an autoencoder reconstruction score with a supervised classifier and exposes a simple review UI for human-in-the-loop labeling.

churn.title: Churn Prediction Warehouse
churn.description: A dbt-modeled warehouse feeding a churn model with automated retraining.
churn.details: Raw data is transformed with dbt into clean analytics models, features are materialized in SQL, and a scikit-learn model is retrained on a schedule via Airflow. Predictions are written back for downstream CRM activation.

infra-platform.title: Internal ML Platform
infra-platform.description: A self-service cloud platform for data teams to deploy models and jobs.
infra-platform.details: A Kubernetes-based platform provisioned with Terraform, giving teams reproducible environments, secrets management, and one-click deployment of containerized workloads on AWS.

cicd-mlops.title: MLOps CI/CD Toolkit
cicd-mlops.description: Reusable pipelines for testing, building, and shipping ML services.
cicd-mlops.details: GitHub Actions workflows that lint, test, build Docker images, and deploy to Kubernetes, with Airflow integration for scheduled retraining and rollback strategies for models.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Filter by domain to highlight related skills. Select a sphere to see details.

pytorch.desc: My go-to framework for deep learning. I've trained vision and NLP models with it, from quick prototypes to production inference.

llm.desc: I build retrieval-augmented systems and evaluate them rigorously. Prompting, chunking, and grounding are part of my daily work.

sklearn.desc: The workhorse for classical ML. I rely on it for strong baselines, feature pipelines, and interpretable models.

spark.desc: I use Spark for large-scale batch and streaming transformations where a single machine just won't cut it.

airflow.desc: My orchestrator of choice for scheduling, dependencies, and retries across data and ML workflows.

kafka.desc: The backbone of my event-driven pipelines, decoupling producers from consumers at scale.

dbt.desc: I model analytics data with dbt to keep transformations versioned, tested, and documented.

aws.desc: My primary cloud. I deploy pipelines and ML services here with a focus on cost and reliability.

gcp.desc: Used for training and serving vision models, especially during my research-focused work.

docker.desc: Everything I ship is containerized for reproducibility — from training jobs to API services.

k8s.desc: I deploy and scale containerized ML services on Kubernetes with autoscaling and health checks.

terraform.desc: I manage cloud infrastructure as code so environments are reproducible and reviewable.

python.desc: My main language for data, ML, and backend work. It's where most of my code lives.

sql.desc: I think in SQL for analytics modeling, feature engineering, and warehouse work.

cpp.desc: Used for performance-critical components and during my systems-oriented coursework and research.

fastapi.desc: My preferred way to expose ML models as fast, typed, async APIs.

react.desc: I build lightweight UIs and dashboards to make models and metrics accessible to humans.

rag-eval.title: Evaluating RAG Systems Beyond Accuracy
rag-eval.abstract: A practical framework for measuring faithfulness, retrieval quality, and answer usefulness in production RAG.

streaming-report.title: Designing Resilient Streaming Pipelines
streaming-report.abstract: Lessons learned building Kafka and Spark pipelines that survive failures and backpressure at scale.

ai-ethics.title: On the Responsibility of AI Engineers
ai-ethics.abstract: A reflection on the ethical choices we make when shipping models that affect real people.

anomaly-detection.title: Hybrid Anomaly Detection for Vision
anomaly-detection.abstract: Combining reconstruction-based and supervised approaches for robust industrial defect detection.
